channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Musk and Altman to face off over the future of OpenAI in trial
item.description: In Oakland, a case is getting underway in which Elon Musk and Sam Altman will face off over the future of OpenAI, one of the world's most influential AI companies.
item.content:encoded: In Oakland, a case is getting underway in which Elon Musk and Sam Altman will face off over the future of OpenAI, one of the world's most influential AI companies.
item.dc:creator: Rachael Myrow

item.title: As school choice expands in Iowa, one district is in a crisis from losing students
item.description: With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.
item.content:encoded: With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Illinois governor weighs in on Operation Midway Blitz hearings
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker regarding the public hearings of the Illinois Accountability Commission investigating federal immigration enforcement in Chicago.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker regarding the public hearings of the Illinois Accountability Commission investigating federal immigration enforcement in Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Marquez Janse

item.title: Shoes launch sub-two hour marathon times
item.description: Two marathoners clocked in under two hours in London. We talk to one researcher about the role of shoes in making the impossible possible.
item.content:encoded: Two marathoners clocked in under two hours in London. We talk to one researcher about the role of shoes in making the impossible possible.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Pierre

item.title: Can the famous Rocky statue punch through political disagreements?
item.description: Philadelphia's Rocky statue gets as many visitors as the Statue of Liberty. Now the Philadelphia Museum of Art is trying to use it to create dialogue between people who disagree.
item.content:encoded: Philadelphia's Rocky statue gets as many visitors as the Statue of Liberty. Now the Philadelphia Museum of Art is trying to use it to create dialogue between people who disagree.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kramon

item.title: FCC orders early license renewal for ABC stations following Kimmel's first lady joke
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission has ordered Disney's ABC to seek early broadcast license renewals for the eight TV stations it owns amid backlash over Jimmy Kimmel's joke about Melania Trump.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Communications Commission has ordered Disney's ABC to seek early broadcast license renewals for the eight TV stations it owns amid backlash over Jimmy Kimmel's joke about Melania Trump.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: How the city with the most to lose in the Colorado River crisis is trying to adapt
item.description: Record low winter snows mean insufficient water in the Colorado River. Here's how a city that's first in line to be cut off is handling it.
item.content:encoded:
Record low winter snows mean insufficient water in the Colorado River. Here's how a city that's first in line to be cut off is handling it.

(Image credit: Alex Hager)
item.dc:creator: Alex Hager

item.title: Colombia's election takes a tense turn with wave of pre-election violence
item.description: Colombia's presidential race unfolds amid weekend bomb attacks and rising fears of political violence ahead of May's vote. The left-wing frontrunner is trailed by a fragmented right-wing opposition.
item.content:encoded: Colombia's presidential race unfolds amid weekend bomb attacks and rising fears of political violence ahead of May's vote. The left-wing frontrunner is trailed by a fragmented right-wing opposition.
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: Mali's Russian-backed troops retreat as militant offensive spreads
item.description: Mali's worsening crisis sees jihadist and separatist advances, deepening instability and increasing fears of wider Sahel regional collapse.
item.content:encoded: Mali's worsening crisis sees jihadist and separatist advances, deepening instability and increasing fears of wider Sahel regional collapse.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Akinwotu

item.title: King Charles urges 'reconciliation' in address to U.S. Congress amid Trump tensions
item.description: In an address to the U.S. Congress, King Charles told lawmakers that "time and again, our two countries have always found ways to come together," and called for "reconciliation and renewal."
item.content:encoded: In an address to the U.S. Congress, King Charles told lawmakers that "time and again, our two countries have always found ways to come together," and called for "reconciliation and renewal."
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez
